entry.title: Washington Post: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Biden and China’s Xi speak amid Taiwan tension
  • Climate change is killing more elephants than poaching, Kenyan officials say
  • New WHO monkeypox advice urges men who have sex with men to limit partners
  • Saudi crown prince to meet Macron, as Khashoggi group urges prosecution in France
  • Nuclear threat higher now than in Cold War, British official warns
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia noncommittal on Griner deal; air strikes reported outside Kyiv
  • Russia: No deal yet on releasing Americans Brittney Griner, Paul Whelan
  • Proposal for Griner, Whelan follows history of U.S.-Russia prisoner swaps
  • Ancient Europeans were lactose intolerant. They drank milk anyway, study finds.
  • Morocco builds ‘ecosystem of repression’ to quash dissent, report says
  • Five dead, dozens injured as 7.0-magnitude earthquake hits Philippines
  • Africa is being pushed to take sides in the Ukraine war
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: U.S. made ‘substantial proposal’ for release of Griner and Whelan
  • Accused Honduran drug cartel matriarch appears in U.S. court
  • U.S. offers deal to Russia to free Brittney Griner as she testifies in Moscow
  • Who is Viktor Bout, Russian arms dealer eyed in rumored prisoner swap?
  • Violence against women rampant under Taliban, new report finds
  • Hungary’s Viktor Orban faces outrage after saying Europeans shouldn’t become ‘mixed race’
  • Emu and chef help stop driver fleeing crash site
  • Pentagon calls out China’s military threats as Taiwan tensions worsen
  • Amnesty urges Egypt to free prominent activist, allow access
  • In Mexico, killing of young doctor leads to protest
  • 5 dead, 66 migrants rescued from waters near Puerto Rico
  • Energy on the table, Macron hosts Saudi prince for dinner
  • Russia attacks Kyiv area for the first time in weeks
  • The Claremont Institute triumphed in the Trump years. Then came Jan. 6.
  • Girl, 12, challenges W.Va. lawmakers on abortion: ‘What about my life?’
  • Female inmates raped after guard sold key to their cells, lawsuit says
  • In viral ad, doctor calls Texas governor to get permission for abortion
  • Employees at a fruit stand found a wallet. It was William Shatner’s.
  • How 80 dogs were saved: ‘We’ve done a lot of rescues ... but never one like this’
  • Highland Park shooting suspect indicted on more than 100 felony counts
  • Companies made more than $1B selling powerful guns to civilians, report says
  • Uvalde school board calls on Texas to raise minimum age for assault weapons
  • They lowered her casket into the ground. It wasn’t their mother inside.
  • As Mega Millions hits $1 billion, winning doesn’t mean a happy ending
  • Ex-officers sentenced for violating George Floyd’s civil rights
  • Post Politics Now: ‘No surprise’ that the economy is slowing, country on ‘right path,' Biden says
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says he’s ‘feeling great’ after testing negative for the coronavirus
  • Co-defendant in Jan. 6 Sicknick assault case pleads guilty
  • The Georgia criminal investigation into Trump and his allies, explained
  • U.S. economy shrinks again in second quarter, reviving recession fears
  • JetBlue agrees to buy Spirit in $3.8 billion deal
  • Mortgage rates are brought down by recession fears
  • One woman sharing workplace bathrooms with 50 men
  • The MTV VMAs debut category for best virtual and video game concerts
  • Your phone has tools to help you use it less. They work.
  • Environmentally friendly apartments coming to Hyattsville, Md.
  • Manchin’s Shock Gives Clean Tech a Welcome Jolt
  • To Save the Planet, Poor Nations Need to Get Paid
  • New Budget Deal Would Be a Big Win for Congress — and the Country
  • Jack Ma Surrenders on Ant Financial. Smart Move.
  • Why Poor Nations Now Face a Pandemic Debt Crunch
  • What’s Driving the US-China Spat Over Audits and Delisting
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution: QuickTake
  • Why Blackouts Are Still Crippling South Africa
  • Russia locked up Vladimir Kara-Murza for telling the truth about Ukraine
  • Despite evidence of innocence, officials leave Alabama man on death row
  • A Trump nomination is in no one’s interest. Democrats included.
  • Here’s a test to see whether Supreme Court justices are above the law
  • Here’s how states can empower citizens to help combat gun violence
  • California wants to help me buy a house. Here’s why that’s a bad idea.
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Consumer spending continues to shift from goods to services.
  • Highlights From Today’s G.D.P. Report
  • Surprise Deal Would Be Most Ambitious Climate Action Undertaken by U.S.
  • Manchin, in Reversal, Agrees to Quick Action on Climate and Tax Plan

  • Crops ‘Stored Everywhere’: Ukraine’s Harvest Piles Up

  • The U.S. Senate passes a resolution seeking to label Russia as a sponsor of terrorism.
  • Britain’s Power Grid Warns of Tight Energy Supply

  • Biden Is Facing Crisis After Crisis. But Are They Emergencies?

  • Alex Lasry Ends His Senate Bid in Wisconsin
  • JetBlue and Spirit Airlines Announce Merger Plan
  • G.D.P. Report Shows U.S. Economy Shrank Again: Live Updates
  • Federal Reserve Makes Another Supersized Rate Increase to Tame Inflation
  • F.T.C. Sues to Block Meta’s Virtual Reality Deal as It Confronts Big Tech
  • Meta Reports First Revenue Decline and 36 Percent Profit Drop

  • Shell Reports Record $11.5 Billion Profit

  • Ford’s Quarterly Profit Is Up Nearly 20% From a Year Earlier

  • Lufthansa Cancels Nearly All Flights in Frankfurt and Munich, Stranding 130,000

  • The Default Tech Settings You Should Turn Off Right Away
  • So Much Tech. So Few Winners.
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Guardian: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war latest: five killed and 25 injured in Russian strike on Kropyvnytskyi
  • Rishi Sunak admits ‘I’m the underdog’ ahead of Tory leadership hustings with Liz Truss – UK politics live
  • Joe Manchin makes U-turn on tax and climate bill as US edges closer to recession – live

  • Wuthering Heights and Lionhearts – take the Thursday quiz Kate Bush special
  • Keep cool and carry on: readers’ favourite trips to escape the heat
  • What has happened to the Labour party that it can't stand up for labour? | John McDonnell
  • Reader, I married one – and moved there. How Neighbours gave me a deep Australian kink
  • Russian forces fire barrage of missiles at northern Ukraine from Belarus
  • The heartbreak of becoming a liberal in a conservative family
  • ‘It’s plunder’: Mexico desperate for water while drinks companies use billions of litres
  • The 'sadmin' after my mother’s death was hard enough – then I encountered Vodafone | George Monbiot
  • ‘All bodies are beach bodies’: Spain’s equality ministry launches summer campaign
  • Snipped in solidarity: the American men getting vasectomies after Roe – while they can
  • Bicycle graveyards: why do so many bikes end up underwater?

  • Sri Lanka extends state of emergency as police round up protest leaders

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 28 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 28 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-28 15:08PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • As many as 3 people injured by giant video screen falling onto stage at Mirror concert in Hong Kong
  • How Hong Kong became a cultural and commercial arts hub for Asia
  • Foodpanda to give HK$75,000 to family of Hong Kong rider killed in road accident
  • Hong Kong should align regulations with mainland China to promote health technology in Greater Bay Area, experts say
  • Hang Lung’s chairman warns of turbulent times ahead for Hong Kong’s real estate as city finds itself squeezed between US and China
  • Hong Kong home prices: worst yet to come, with up to 30 per cent slump a possibility as market enters decline
  • In a class of its own? Hong Kong University of Science and Technology to launch virtual reality lessons in bid to create metaverse campus
  • Former Hong Kong opposition district councillor jailed for insulting police at protest
  • Labour chief rejects pleas to make heatstroke an occupational disease after Hong Kong swelters in record temperatures
  • Hong Kong Financial Secretary Paul Chan says global rate rises, recession will hurt exports, but consumption vouchers will offset impact
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee to head command structure to coordinate 13 task forces against rise in coronavirus cases
  • More than 100 customers show up at Hong Kong’s Happy Cake Shop to bid farewell to popular bakery ahead of closure
  • Teacher exodus leaves Hong Kong schools scrambling to hire fresh graduates, fill vacancies for senior positions
  • Sell Hong Kong stocks when the likes of HSBC raise prime rates, historical data suggests
  • Hong Kong’s online retail market has become too crowded for e-commerce giant Alibaba, which is closing its local Tmall site
  • Hong Kong raises base rate to 2.75 per cent in lockstep with US Fed’s 75-basis point hike
  • Hong Kong’s brightest should explore mainland science and technology opportunities to reach their full potential
  • Classic Hong Kong bakery Happy Cake Shop to close after 45 years in business as landlord takes back premises
  • UN human rights watchdog urges Hong Kong to take ‘concrete steps’ to repeal national security law; government slams ‘unfair’ criticism
  • Risk of accidental US-China conflict over Taiwan could leave Japan badly exposed
  • Stay hydrated any place, any time: Chinese scientists develop a low-cost sponge that can suck water from air
  • China-US relations: Xi and Biden move to calm tensions during fifth call
  • China’s Politburo ‘insists’ on zero-Covid, omits mention of GDP growth target
  • China ramps up subsidies to lure chip firms to Hengqin, an island near Macau, turning it into a major semiconductor outpost
  • Japanese lawmakers visit Taiwan for talks on how to prepare for conflict
  • US, China urged to be ‘responsible’ global economic leaders in wake of Fed rate hike
  • China’s anti-corruption watchdog investigates minister leading tech drive
  • TikTok’s global expansion under a cloud as it faces renewed scrutiny over data privacy, China links
  • UK national security adviser warns of risk of miscalculating into nuclear war with China, Russia
  • China prods South Korea to keep promise on US-made anti-missile systems
  • Taiwan ‘will have to bear the wrath of Beijing’ if Nancy Pelosi goes ahead with visit, analysts say
  • Manhunt under way after 3 killed in gun attack in southwest China
  • Shimao, once hailed as one of China’s stronger developers, sued by UOB over terms of US$1.3 billion loan
  • China’s AI-driven power grid can recover from a blackout in 3 seconds
  • Uygur separatist group rebuilds bases in Afghanistan even as China-Taliban ties grow
  • Joe Biden to bring up Taiwan, ‘excessive’ South China Sea claims in call with Xi Jinping, White House says
  • China, South Korea renew service sector talks, opening up a ‘win-win for both economies’
  • US Senate passes bill providing US$52 billion in subsidies to semiconductor industry
  • Malaysia bans party-hopping lawmakers to ease political turmoil
  • Malaysia’s Najib Razak hails Indonesia’s ‘proactive steps’ to deepen China ties
  • After decriminalising cannabis, Thailand may gamble on casinos to lure more tourists to revive pandemic-hit economy
  • 1MDB-linked hip-hop artist Pras Michel sells stake in Fugees Library to Apollo-backed firm
  • Will South Korea join US-led chip alliance ‘Chip 4’, and risk business ties with China?
  • North Korea’s Kim Jong-un threatens to use nuclear weapons amid tensions with US, South Korea
  • Fearing US sanctions, Philippines scraps US$227 million deal to buy 16 Russian helicopters
  • Two charged in murder of Ripudaman Singh Malik, Canadian Sikh acquitted in Air India bombing
  • How instability in Afghanistan raises the global risk of narco-terrorism
  • Saudi crown prince stays in ‘world’s most expensive home’ ahead of Macron meeting
  • US economy shrinks in second quarter, raising recession fears
  • Wife of Russian who inspired Lord of War says arms dealer unaware of US prisoner swap offer
  • Sea mine risk slows rush to extract grain from Ukraine
  • Apple nabs key Lamborghini executive to supercharge its self-driving electric car project after years of setbacks
  • Reduce number of sex partners to avoid monkeypox, WHO urges after declaring global emergency
  • Nasa details plans to bring back Mars rock samples by 2033, two years behind China
  • Joe Biden hails Democrats’ breakthrough on health, climate spending bill
  • In Britain, talk of Boris Johnson’s return to No 10 before he’s even left
  • Ukraine counterattacks and warns Russians in occupied Kherson: ‘surrender or be destroyed’
  • Former Republicans and Democrats form third US political party, co-chaired by Andrew Yang
  • Blinken says US offered Russia ‘substantial’ deal to free Americans Brittney Griner, Paul Whelan
  • US Federal Reserve unleashes another big rate hike in bid to curb inflation
  • US gun makers made over US$1 billion from AR-15 sales in past decade
  • Macau invites casino operators to apply for licence renewals until September 24 to secure spots in the world’s gambling hub
  • Why betting against the Hong Kong dollar peg is a fool’s errand
  • Victor Li’s CK Asset submits bid for China Evergrande’s flagship Hong Kong building in lifeline for distressed developer
  • Alibaba, Meituan are top picks at Daiwa in sector upgrade as internet stocks to benefit most from China stimulus
  • Hong Kong stocks weaken with AIA, banks, developers under pressure as rate hike seen weighing on shrinking economy
  • New York-listed Chinese cloud services provider Kingsoft Cloud files to go public in Hong Kong
  • How Southeast Asia can foster an inclusive economic recovery
  • Ex-porn site owner who used nude photos of non-consenting subjects profiled in new Netflix documentary
  • K-drama review – Link: Eat, Love, Kill, Disney+ romcom-thriller mash-up, succeeds thanks to Moon Ga-young/Yeo Jin-goo chemistry
  • 5 new hotels in Japan, in Kyoto, Niseko and Hakone, being opened by Singapore’s Banyan Tree Group, its first in the country
  • Peking duck, paella, May Chow’s baos – favourite Hong Kong restaurants of a food and beverage marketer
  • NewJeans, new K-pop group, have YouTube, TikTok abuzz – here’s everything you need to know about them
  • How your liver keeps you healthy and how hepatitis vaccinations and a good diet can protect it
  • Tomb of the River movie review: generic Korean crime thriller starring Yoo Oh-seong, Jang Hyuk mixes bravado and bloodshed
  • A holiday in Saudi Arabia? But what about its human rights record, you ask. If you want squeaky clean, not many countries will make your itinerary
  • How to create the best skincare routine for summer: remember sunscreen on your head, lip care to avoid burns
  • Autistic genius lawyer in Netflix K-drama makes it a global sensation – why Extraordinary Attorney Woo, starring Park Eun-bin, gets everything right
  • Has silicon won over Swiss watchmakers? Rolex, Jaeger-LeCoultre, Vacheron Constantin, Girard-Perregaux, IWC and Panerai are just a few brands on board with the revolutionary technology
  • Meet Antoine Arnault, billionaire Bernard Arnault’s eldest son: he runs Loro Piana and Berluti, is married to supermodel Natalia Vodianova and is a world-class poker player – could he inherit LVMH?
  • Why luxury travellers are getting up close with nature – with five-star amenities: Four Seasons Tented Camp, Banyan Tree Group and Bensley Collection offer resort experiences in the wild
  • 6 Korean stars who survived being cancelled by scandals, from AOA’s Jimin and Stray Kids’ Kim Woojin, to Red Velvet’s Irene, Seo Ye-ji and Singles’ Inferno’s Song Ji-a
  • Hong Kong billionaire Pansy Ho at 60: from friendships with Canto-pop icons Anita Mui and Leslie Cheung, to taking over her casino tycoon father Stanley Ho’s business empire
  • STYLE Edit: Richard Mille unveils the world’s thinnest watch – the RM UP-01 Ferrari is a 1.75mm timepiece that perfectly fuses the craftsmanship of luxury watchmaker and carmaker
  • The fabulous life (and wardrobe) of Cardi B’s daughter Kulture: from Dior and Chanel shopping sprees with mum, to Swarovski-encrusted Hermès Birkins, a Rolls-Royce car seat and her own Richard Mille
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
